texto: In the libraryI search for a good book.We have many books,says Mrs. Rose, the librarian,and ALL of them are good.Of course she says that. It's her job.But do I want to read about TrucksTrains and Transport? Or evenHorsesHouses and Hyenas?In the fiction cornerthere are pink boksfull of princessesand girls who want to be princessesand black booksabout bad boysand brave boysand brawny boys.Where is the bookabout a girlwhose poems don't rhymeand whose Granny is fading?Pearl, says Mrs. Rose, the bell has rung.I go back to classempty-handedempty headedempty-hearted.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Sally Murphy que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “In the libraryI search for a good book.We have many books,says Mrs. Rose, the librarian,and ALL o…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, grief, libraries, com foco em luto e dia-chuvoso.
